Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel-Civil
Off Calendar Jul-09-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Elizabeth Button, Moving Party seeking to be relieved as counsel to Maisha Hicks (via Zoom).
Maisha Hicks, Administrator (via Zoom).
Constance Figuers for Probate Advance LLC, Assignee (via Zoom).

Ms. Hicks confirmed that neither she nor her family objected to Ms. Button's withdrawal and stated that they were finalizing retention of new counsel, Sheila Robello. The Court took the Ms. Button's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel under submission with the intent to grant.

As to the closing of the estate, Ms. Hicks informed the Court that she and her family had coordinated with a fiduciary, who is currently working on the accounting. Ms. Hicks stated that the accounting should be completed by the end of the week. She also explained that the family has been personally covering the mortgage and rehabilitating the estate property in preparation for distribution. A lender is in place to assist with final distribution once the accounting is completed and approved.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to August 12, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Oct-18-2022 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-18-2022 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-18-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Button, via Zoom, for and with Maisha Hicks, Petitioner; Thomas Wrobel, via Zoom, for Dudley Burns, IV, Petitioner.

The court notes that Mr. Burns is not present this morning. The court denies Mr. Burns' Petition for Letters of Administration in open court and takes Mr. Wrobel's Motion to be Relieved as counsel under submission. The court confirms that no one appeared to object to Maisha Hicks' Petition for Letters of Administration. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration. Maisha Hicks is appointed Administrator of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $1,400,000. Frank Mahoney III is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Nov-07-2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-20-2022 Continued To Oct-18-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Button, for and with Maisha Hicks, Petitioner; Thomas Wrobel, via Zoom, for Dudley A. Burns IV, Petitioner.

The court acknowledges Mr. Wrobel's Notice of Motion and Motion to be Relieved as counsel pending in the court's e-filing queue. Mr. Wrobel reports as to the issues which led him to file the Motion including, but not limited to, a breakdown in communication and attorney/client relationship. Ms. Button reports that the parties had a tentative agreement with Mr. Burns but that he was unresponsive thereafter.

After additional discussion, the matters are continued to Oct-18-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court advises Mr. Wrobel that it will be instructing the filing clerks to schedule his Motion to be Relieved as Counsel to be heard alongside the pending petitions if after review, it is determined that his papers are ready to file.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-23-2022 Continued To Sep-20-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-23-2022 Continued To Sep-20-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Button, via Zoom, for and with Maisha Hicks, Petitioner; Thomas Wrobel, via Zoom, for Dudley Burns, Petitioner.

Mr. Wrobel states that his client has priority for appointment but that there have been discussions regarding the competing petitions. Ms. Button reports that the main asset of the estate is the real property and advises of concerns regarding how Mr. Wrobel's client is handling the property and of the urgency to move this matter forward and appoint an Administrator. The court offers its position and confirms that Mr. Dudley does have priority for appointment over her client. Mr. Wrobel suggests a continuance of this matter to allow the parties to continue to meet and confer. Ms. Button advises that previous meetings have not been productive and is not confident in Mr. Burns' ability to act as Administrator if he is appointed. The court states that it intends to continue this matter and advises counsel that if a resolution is not possible prior to the next calling of this matter, counsel should be ready to discuss plans for case management.

The matters are continued to Sep-20-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.
